title: NOC 70011 – Home building and renovation managers | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Home Building and Renovation Managers (NOC 70011) are the operational experts navigating a complex residential landscape. While Canada continues to face an acute housing shortage, the 2026 market is characterized by high construction costs and a shift toward energy-efficient retrofitting and high-density multiplexes. These managers do more than supervise sites; they manage the entire lifecycle of a residential project, from initial client consultation and bidding to coordinating specialized sub-trades and ensuring compliance with the latest net-zero building codes. As the industry moves toward sustainable "Green Building," managers with LEED or similar certifications are seeing the highest demand.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category0 (Management occupations)Major Group70 – Middle management occupations in trades and transportationSub-Major Group700 – Middle management occupations in trades and transportation

duties: Project Management: Planning, organizing, and evaluating the activities of a residential home building or renovating company.
Bid Preparation: Preparing detailed bids and cost estimates for new construction or renovation projects.
Subcontractor Management: Selecting, hiring, and supervising trade subcontractors (plumbers, electricians, etc.) to ensure specialized work meets standards.
Client Consultation: Consulting with customers, architects, and engineers regarding project plans, specifications, and design changes.
Quality Assurance: Inspecting work performed by subcontractors to ensure total quality and conformity with municipal building codes and safety regulations.
employment_requirements: Education: A university degree or college diploma in Construction Technology or Civil Engineering is common.
Experience: Extensive experience in the home construction industry (usually 5–10 years), including time spent as a general supervisor.
Professional Certification: While not mandatory in all provinces, Red Seal certification in a trade or LEED certification is a massive competitive asset in 2026.
Business Licensing: Managers must often hold specific municipal or provincial builder licenses (e.g., BC Housing or Tarion in Ontario).
salary_prospects: Note: For the 74% who are self-employed, total earnings vary significantly based on project volume and margins.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Good, with significant regional variations influenced by interest rates and provincial housing policies:

Ontario (Toronto/GTA): Good prospects. Despite a cooling in new high-rise starts, the renovation and custom home market remains resilient. Over 31,000 people work in this NOC in the Toronto region alone.

British Columbia (Vancouver): Strong demand. While new starts have dropped to historically weak levels, the focus has shifted to the massive demand for secondary suites and multiplexes permitted under new provincial zoning laws.

The Prairies (Alberta & Saskatchewan): Very Good. Unlike the coastal provinces, Alberta and Saskatchewan are seeing housing starts stay above historical averages, driven by relative affordability and population growth.
Nova Scotia: Strong Demand. Home building managers are currently listed as a priority occupation for the province’s Skilled Worker stream in early 2026.
Why Work in Canada as a Home Building Manager?
Entrepreneurial Flexibility: Approximately 74% of managers in this NOC are self-employed, offering significant control over project selection and earnings.
Essential Service Impact: Lead projects that directly address the national housing crisis, specializing in modern, affordable, and sustainable dwelling solutions.
Digital Integration: Gain experience with 2026-grade project management software, digital client portals, and real-time budget tracking tools.
Priority for PR: As a TEER 0 management role, it qualifies for all major Express Entry categories and is frequently targeted by OINP and BC PNP draws.
pathways: OINP Employer Job Offer (Foreign Worker Stream): In early 2026, Ontario issued targeted invitations for NOC 70011 with scores starting at 53–57, specifically in Northern and Southwestern Ontario regions.
BC PNP Construction Stream: A priority pathway for managers with a job offer from a BC-based home building or renovation firm.
Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker): As a TEER 0 role, it is eligible for general draws. Managers with Canadian experience can target the CEC stream.
Nova Scotia Skilled Worker: The province currently identifies Home Building Managers as an "In-Demand" construction occupation for direct provincial nomination.
